# PIPA Privacy Policy Template

Last reviewed: 2026-04-21

Use this as a board/staff draft for a BC society or non-profit. Replace all
bracketed text before adopting it. This template is not legal advice.

## Privacy Policy

### 1. Organization

[Legal organization name] is a British Columbia [society / non-profit /
student society / other organization]. We collect, use, disclose, store, and
dispose of personal information in accordance with British Columbia's Personal
Information Protection Act (PIPA) and other applicable laws.

Organization contact:

- Legal name: [legal name]
- Incorporation number: [if applicable]
- Mailing address: [mailing address]
- General contact: [email]

### 2. Privacy Officer

The privacy officer is responsible for privacy questions, access and correction
requests, privacy complaints, and maintaining this policy.

- Privacy officer title/name: [role or name]
- Email: [privacy email]
- Mailing address: [mailing address, if different]

### 3. Personal Information We Collect

We collect only the personal information that is reasonable for our purposes.
Depending on the activity, this may include:

- name and contact information;
- membership or eligibility information;
- director, officer, staff, contractor, and volunteer records;
- event registration and attendance information;
- communication preferences and mailing list records;
- payment, reimbursement, grant, or funding records;
- application, intake, or service-request information;
- complaint, dispute, or access-request records;
- records needed for meetings, elections, referenda, filings, insurance,
  accounting, or legal compliance; and
- technical information generated when people use our websites, forms, or
  systems.

We do not intentionally collect sensitive personal information unless it is
needed for a specific purpose and the collection is reasonable in the
circumstances.

### 4. Why We Collect Personal Information

We may collect personal information to:

- administer the organization and its governance;
- maintain required society records;
- manage memberships, eligibility, and voting where applicable;
- communicate with members, directors, staff, volunteers, applicants, funders,
  partners, and the public;
- run meetings, elections, referenda, events, programs, and services;
- process payments, reimbursements, grants, donations, invoices, payroll, or
  accounting records;
- recruit, onboard, manage, and support staff, contractors, directors, and
  volunteers;
- respond to questions, complaints, access requests, and correction requests;
- protect the safety, security, and legal interests of the organization and
  individuals; and
- meet legal, regulatory, audit, funding, insurance, and reporting obligations.

### 5. Consent and Notice

When we collect personal information directly from an individual, we explain the
purpose for collection at or before the time of collection unless the purpose is
obvious and the individual voluntarily provides the information for that
purpose.

We collect, use, and disclose personal information with consent unless PIPA or
another law authorizes the collection, use, or disclosure without consent.
Consent may be express, implied, deemed, or opt-out depending on the information,
the context, and legal requirements.

An individual may withdraw consent by contacting the privacy officer, subject to
legal, contractual, funding, governance, audit, or operational limits.

### 6. How We Use Personal Information

We use personal information only for the purposes for which it was collected,
for purposes reasonably related to those purposes, or for other purposes
authorized by law.

Access is limited to people who need the information for their role, such as
authorized directors, officers, staff, contractors, volunteers, committee
members, or service providers.

### 7. Disclosure

We may disclose personal information when:

- the individual consents;
- disclosure is needed for the purpose for which the information was collected;
- disclosure is needed to a service provider acting for the organization;
- disclosure is required for governance, accounting, audit, insurance, funding,
  payroll, legal, regulatory, or filing purposes;
- disclosure is required or authorized by law; or
- disclosure is needed to respond to an emergency, safety issue, legal claim,
  investigation, or complaint.

We do not sell personal information.

### 8. Service Providers

We may use service providers for email, cloud storage, accounting, forms,
payments, websites, communications, voting, records management, security,
document storage, and other operations.

When service providers handle personal information for us, we take reasonable
steps to ensure they protect the information and use it only for authorized
purposes.

### 9. Member Records and Institution-Held Data

[Choose and adapt one option.]

Option A - society-controlled member records:

The organization maintains its own member register and related member records.
Access to member records is restricted to authorized people and handled under
the applicable statute, bylaws, and privacy rules.

Option B - institution-held or partially available member records:

Some member or eligibility information may be held by [university / parent
institution / other body]. The organization is responsible for personal
information it collects or controls. Institution-held records are not treated as
organization-controlled unless the organization has access to them, a copy of
them, a contractual right to obtain them, a shared system, or the practical or
legal ability to direct their use or disclosure.

The organization maintains the member records it can lawfully maintain and keeps
a separate member-data access gap memo where the institution does not provide a
full member list.

### 10. Safeguards

We protect personal information using safeguards appropriate to the sensitivity
and amount of information. These may include:

- limited access by role;
- password protection and multi-factor authentication where practical;
- restricted cloud folders and document permissions;
- locked physical storage where paper records are used;
- secure deletion or disposal;
- staff, director, or volunteer training;
- service-provider controls; and
- review of access after role changes.

### 11. Retention and Disposal

We keep personal information only as long as needed for the purpose for which it
was collected, or as long as needed for legal, governance, funding, audit,
insurance, tax, accounting, dispute-resolution, or business purposes.

If personal information is used to make a decision that directly affects an
individual, we retain that information for at least one year after the decision
so the individual has a reasonable opportunity to request access.

When retention is no longer needed, we securely destroy records or remove the
means by which the information can be associated with an identifiable
individual.

### 12. Access and Correction Requests

Individuals may ask for access to their own personal information under the
organization's control. They may also ask how their information has been used
and disclosed, and may request correction if they believe it is inaccurate.

Requests should be made in writing to the privacy officer. We will make a
reasonable effort to assist and will respond within the timelines required by
PIPA unless an extension or exception applies.

If requested records are held by another organization and are not under our
control, we will explain that and, where appropriate, identify the organization
the requester may need to contact.

### 13. Complaints

Individuals may contact the privacy officer with questions or complaints about
the organization's handling of personal information.

The privacy officer will:

- acknowledge the complaint;
- review the relevant facts and records;
- involve appropriate directors, officers, staff, or service providers;
- respond with findings or next steps; and
- keep a record of the complaint and outcome.

If an individual is not satisfied, they may contact the Office of the
Information and Privacy Commissioner for British Columbia.

### 14. Breaches

A privacy breach includes unauthorized access, collection, use, disclosure,
copying, modification, loss, theft, disposal, or destruction of personal
information.

If a suspected breach occurs, the organization will:

- contain the breach where possible;
- assess what happened and what information is involved;
- evaluate risk of harm;
- notify affected individuals, regulators, institutions, funders, insurers, or
  others where required or appropriate;
- document the response; and
- take steps to reduce the chance of recurrence.

### 15. Review

This policy will be reviewed at least annually and whenever the organization:

- changes how it collects, uses, stores, or discloses personal information;
- adopts a new system or service provider;
- starts a new program, election, referendum, intake, or service;
- has a privacy complaint or breach; or
- receives new legal, regulatory, funder, or institutional requirements.

Policy adopted by: [board / executive / authorized officer]

Adoption date: [YYYY-MM-DD]

Last review date: [YYYY-MM-DD]

Next review date: [YYYY-MM-DD]

## Source Notes

This template was prepared using the following public resources:

- BC PIPA, especially sections 4, 5, 6-10, 23-24, 27-30, 33-35.
- BC government guidance on protecting personal information.
- BC OIPC guidance for private organizations.
- BC OIPC guidance on developing a privacy policy under PIPA.
- BC OIPC PrivacyRight webinars and privacy management resources.
